questions.question: Which of the following is a type of multiple-processor scheduling?
options: First-Come-First-Served (FCFS)
Round-Robin Scheduling (RRS)
Priority Scheduling (PS)
Dynamic Priority Scheduling (DPS)
questions.explanation: Round-Robin Scheduling (RRS) is a type of multiple-processor scheduling where each process is assigned a time slice and executed in a fixed order.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of the Multi-Queue algorithm?
options: To minimize the overhead of context switching
To optimize the utilization of multiple processors
To improve the responsiveness of the system
To reduce the priority of high-priority processes
questions.explanation: The primary goal of the Multi-Queue algorithm is to optimize the utilization of multiple processors by assigning processes to multiple queues based on their priority and availability.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of the Deadline Scheduling Algorithm?
options: Each process is assigned a unique queue
Each process is assigned a unique time slice
Each process is assigned a deadline
Each process is assigned a priority
questions.explanation: Each process in the Deadline Scheduling Algorithm is assigned a deadline, which is the time by which the process must complete execution.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of the Least-Latency-First (LLF) scheduling algorithm?
options: It minimizes the overhead of context switching
It optimizes the utilization of multiple processors
It reduces the priority of high-priority processes
It minimizes the response time of the system
questions.explanation: The main advantage of the LLF scheduling algorithm is that it minimizes the response time of the system by giving priority to processes that require the least amount of time to complete.

questions.question: Which of the following is a disadvantage of the Priority Scheduling Algorithm?
options: It optimizes the utilization of multiple processors
It minimizes the overhead of context switching
It reduces the priority of high-priority processes
It can lead to starvation of high-priority processes
questions.explanation: One of the disadvantages of the Priority Scheduling Algorithm is that it can lead to starvation of high-priority processes if the priority of the processes is not properly set.
